top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 119, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic K2 Road Cycle Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
63ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
7,546ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of K2 Road Cycle Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,546ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
K2 Road Cycle Classic's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 119 miles.
At K2 Road Cycle Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
119 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 119 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 119 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is K2 Road Cycle Classic's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 119 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for K2 Road Cycle Classic’s 119 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 119 miles of mixed terrain in Coromandel Peninsula, New Zealand.
